name: Human Insulin (Regular/NPH)
source_note: Data compiled for Arogya knowledge base. Verify against 1mg.com and pharmeasy.in for live updates.
uses: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (when oral agents insufficient)
how_it_works: Binds insulin receptors on target cells (liver, muscle, fat), facilitating glucose uptake, glycogen synthesis, lipogenesis, and protein synthesis while suppressing gluconeogenesis.

serious: Severe hypoglycemia with coma or seizures

food_interactions: Alcohol can cause severe delayed hypoglycemia
Meal timing must match insulin type
what_to_avoid: Always carry glucose tablets or sugar sachet
Rotate injection sites to prevent lipohypertrophy
Regular insulin: inject 30min before meals, Rapid: inject with meals
Store open vial at room temperature (28 days), unopened in fridge
storage: Unopened: refrigerate 2–8°C. Opened: room temperature below 30°C for up to 28 days. Never freeze.
